story.asA: developer integrating Recipe into my photography workflow
story.iWant: a professional CLI with standard command structure and help documentation
story.soThat: I can quickly learn the tool and integrate it into scripts without reading extensive documentation

task.title: Initialize Cobra CLI Structure
task.description: Install Cobra framework, create cmd/cli/ directory structure with main.go, root.go, convert.go files. Implement CLI entry point and root command definition. Run go mod tidy and build CLI.

task.title: Configure Help Text and Documentation
task.description: Write comprehensive Long description for rootCmd including what Recipe does, supported formats, privacy note, and examples. Test help output formatting.

task.title: Implement Convert Command Stub
task.description: Create cmd/cli/convert.go with convertCmd stub that returns "not yet implemented" error. Register command with root. Verify command appears in help.

task.title: Exit Code Handling
task.description: Update cmd/cli/main.go to handle exit codes correctly (0=success, 1=error, 2=usage error). Test exit codes for various scenarios.

task.title: Cross-Platform Build Testing
task.description: Create Makefile target for multi-platform builds (Linux, macOS amd64/arm64, Windows amd64). Build and verify binaries for all platforms.

task.title: Testing and Documentation
task.description: Create cmd/cli/root_test.go with basic tests for root command help and version flag. Update main README.md with CLI build instructions and usage examples. Document in Dev Notes.

criterion.title: Cobra CLI Initialization
criterion.description: Install Cobra framework, initialize CLI structure with cmd/cli/ directory, configure root command with app name "recipe", build CLI binary successfully.
criterion.testApproach: Build CLI with `go build -o recipe cmd/cli/main.go`, run `./recipe` and verify help text displays without errors.

criterion.title: Root Command Configuration
criterion.description: Root command metadata (Use: "recipe", Short/Long descriptions), version information (--version flag), global flags (--verbose, --json).
criterion.testApproach: Run `./recipe --help` to verify help text, `./recipe --version` to verify version string "Recipe CLI v0.1.0".

criterion.title: Help Text and Documentation
criterion.description: Cobra auto-generates help text from command definitions. Custom Long description includes what Recipe does, supported formats (NP3, XMP, lrtemplate), privacy note, GitHub link, and usage examples.
criterion.testApproach: Run `./recipe --help | grep "Supported formats"` and `./recipe --help | grep "Examples"` to verify content.

criterion.title: Exit Code Consistency
criterion.description: Successful execution (no command) exits with code 0, invalid flag exits with code 2, command execution error exits with code 1.
criterion.testApproach: Run `./recipe` and check exit code is 0, run `./recipe --invalid-flag` and check exit code is 2.

criterion.title: Placeholder for Convert Command
criterion.description: Define convertCmd stub in cmd/cli/convert.go with placeholder error "convert command not yet implemented (Story 3-2)". Register with root command.
criterion.testApproach: Run `./recipe convert test.xmp --to np3` and verify error message, check exit code is 1. Run `./recipe --help` and verify convert command is listed.

criterion.title: Cross-Platform Build Verification
criterion.description: CLI builds successfully on Linux (amd64), macOS (amd64, arm64), Windows (amd64). Single binary with no external dependencies, binary size reasonable (<5MB).
criterion.testApproach: Build for all platforms using GOOS/GOARCH env vars or Makefile target, verify binaries exist and are under 5MB.

criterion.title: Go Module Configuration
criterion.description: Cobra dependency added to go.mod (github.com/spf13/cobra v1.8.1+), Go version specified (go 1.25.1), run `go mod tidy` to clean dependencies.
criterion.testApproach: Run `go mod verify` and `go list -m github.com/spf13/cobra` to verify dependency.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: CLI Interface
doc.snippet: CLI module responsibilities: cmd/cli/main.go (entry point), cmd/cli/convert.go (convert command), cmd/cli/root.go (root command, global flags, version). Cobra-based CLI with convert command syntax: recipe convert INPUT --to FORMAT. Exit codes: 0=success, 1=error, 2=usage error.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: CLI Interface
doc.snippet: Cobra command signatures - rootCmd (Use: "recipe", Short: "Convert photo presets between formats"), convertCmd (Use: "convert [input] [output]", Args: cobra.MinimumNArgs(1)). Global flags: --verbose, --json. Version flag accessible via --version.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: CLI Interface
doc.snippet: External dependency: github.com/spf13/cobra v1.8+ for CLI framework (industry standard used by kubectl, hugo, gh). Go Standard Library: os, path/filepath, errors, log/slog, encoding/json, time, runtime, sync.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: CLI Interface
doc.snippet: Help and Version Information - When user runs `recipe --help`, CLI displays comprehensive help text including available commands, flag descriptions and examples, supported formats, exit codes. When user runs `recipe --version`, CLI displays "Recipe CLI v{version}".

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: CLI provides professional command structure following Unix conventions. Syntax: recipe convert INPUT --to FORMAT. Built with Cobra framework. Supports --help and --version flags.

doc.title: Architecture
doc.snippet: Decision: Use Cobra framework for CLI interface. Rationale: Industry standard (kubectl, hugo, gh), active maintenance, auto-generated help text, subcommand hierarchy support. Standard Go project layout with cmd/cli/ entry point.

doc.title: Architecture
doc.snippet: cmd/ directory separates three interfaces (CLI, TUI, WASM) but all use internal/converter. cmd/cli/main.go is CLI entry point. internal/ prevents external imports, enforces API boundaries. Standard Go layout: cmd/{cli,tui,wasm}/ + internal/ + web/.

artifact.reason: Core conversion API that CLI will invoke in Story 3-2. Thread-safe stateless function signature: Convert(input []byte, from, to string) ([]byte, error). Supports auto-detection (from="" parameter).
Format auto-detection function that CLI will use in Story 3-4. Returns format string ("np3", "xmp", "lrtemplate") based on magic bytes and file structure inspection.
Structured error type that CLI will wrap and format for user display. Contains Operation, Format, Cause, and Warnings fields for detailed error reporting.
Central data structure representing all photo editing parameters. CLI doesn't expose this directly but understanding it helps with verbose output planning (Story 3-5).
Module path is github.com/justin/recipe, Go version is 1.25.1. Cobra dependency will be added here.
Reference example of another cmd/ entry point. Shows pattern for separate interface implementations that share internal/converter package.

go.package: CLI framework for command structure, flag parsing, help generation
Error wrapping (already used in converter package)

constraint.description: CLI layer must be thin - all business logic in internal/converter, CLI only handles I/O and formatting
constraint.source: docs/tech-spec-epic-3.md - Services and Modules - Key Design Principles

constraint.description: Stateless operation - each convert call is independent, no global state
constraint.source: docs/tech-spec-epic-3.md - Services and Modules - Key Design Principles

constraint.description: Standard Go project layout: cmd/cli/ for entry point, internal/ for private packages
Exit codes contract: 0=success, 1=error, 2=usage error (Cobra default behavior)
constraint.source: docs/tech-spec-epic-3.md - APIs and Interfaces - Exit Codes table

constraint.description: Unit tests in cmd/cli/root_test.go for root command help display and version flag output
Version hardcoded as "Recipe CLI v0.1.0" for now (future: inject via ldflags during CI/CD build)
constraint.source: docs/stories/3-1-cobra-cli-structure.md - Dev Notes - Key Design Decisions

constraint.description: No config file support - all options via CLI flags (per Tech Spec Out of Scope)

interface.name: converter.Convert
interface.signature: func Convert(input []byte, from, to string) ([]byte, error)
interface.usage: CLI will call this in Story 3-2 after reading input file. Pass empty string for 'from' parameter to enable auto-detection.

interface.name: converter.DetectFormat
interface.usage: CLI will use this in Story 3-4 for explicit format detection. Returns "np3", "xmp", or "lrtemplate".

interface.name: cobra.Command
interface.signature: type Command struct { Use, Short, Long string; Args CobraPositionalArgs; RunE func(*Command, []string) error; ... }
interface.usage: Root command and convert command will be defined as *cobra.Command instances. Use Cobra's AddCommand() to build command hierarchy.

interface.name: rootCmd.Execute
interface.usage: Main function will call rootCmd.Execute() to start CLI processing. Returns error if command fails.

interface.name: os.Exit
interface.usage: Main function uses os.Exit(0) for success, os.Exit(1) for errors. Cobra may use os.Exit(2) for usage errors internally.

standards.para: This story establishes the CLI testing foundation using Go's standard testing package. Unit tests in cmd/cli/root_test.go validate root command help display and version flag output. Table-driven test pattern used throughout project (see internal/converter/converter_test.go for reference). Tests run with `go test ./cmd/cli/...` and coverage reports generated with `go test -cover ./cmd/cli/...`.

idea.description: TestRootCommand - Execute root command with --help flag, verify help text contains "Convert photo presets" and displays without errors
TestVersionFlag - Execute root command with --version flag, verify output contains "v0.1.0" string
TestHelpTextCompleteness - Execute root command with --help, verify output contains "Supported formats", "Examples", "Privacy note" sections
TestExitCodes - Test various scenarios: no args (exit 0), invalid flag (exit 2), not-implemented command (exit 1). Use os/exec to run CLI as subprocess.
TestConvertCommandStub - Execute convert command, verify "not yet implemented" error message and exit code 1. Verify convert appears in --help output.
TestCrossPlatformBuild - Use build tags or CI matrix to build for all platforms (GOOS/GOARCH combinations). Verify binary sizes under 5MB.
TestGoModDependencies - Verify go.mod contains Cobra dependency at correct version. Use `go list -m` to validate.
